# DumbyMap

> DumbyMap converts Markdown text or Semantic HTML into interactive web pages with embedded maps.
> The single entry-point function is `generateMaps(container, options)` exported from `dumbymap.mjs`.
> Maps are declared as YAML inside fenced code blocks tagged `map`.
> Geographic links (`geo:` URIs) connect text to map positions with animated leader lines.

Source: https://github.com/outdoorsafetylab/dumbymap
Live site: https://dumby.org
Examples: https://dumby.org/example/


## Loading the library

The built bundle is a single ES module: `dist/dumbymap.mjs`.
Two CSS files are required: `src/css/style.css` and `src/css/dumbymap.css`.

### From a CDN (jsDelivr)

```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/outdoorsafetylab/dumbymap@master/src/css/style.css">
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/outdoorsafetylab/dumbymap@master/src/css/dumbymap.css">
<script type="module">
  import { generateMaps } from 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/outdoorsafetylab/dumbymap@master/dist/dumbymap.mjs'
  // ... use generateMaps here
</script>
```

### From npm

```
npm install dumbymap
```

```js
import { generateMaps } from 'dumbymap'
```


## Quick start — Markdown input

Put Markdown in a `<script type="text/markdown">` tag and pass it via the `markdown` option.
The container must have `height` set (e.g. `100vh`) so maps have room to render.

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/outdoorsafetylab/dumbymap@master/src/css/style.css">
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/outdoorsafetylab/dumbymap@master/src/css/dumbymap.css">
</head>
<body style="height: 100vh;">
  <div id="app"></div>
  <script type="text/markdown" id="content">
# My Map Page

A map of Taipei.

```map
use: Maplibre
center: [121.5645, 25.0338]
zoom: 12
```
  </script>
  <script type="module">
    import { generateMaps } from 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/outdoorsafetylab/dumbymap@master/dist/dumbymap.mjs'
    generateMaps(document.getElementById('app'), {
      markdown: document.getElementById('content').textContent
    })
  </script>
</body>
</html>
```


## Quick start — Semantic HTML input

If you already have HTML, just call `generateMaps(container)` with no `markdown` option.
DumbyMap will discover the content holder automatically (searches for `<main>`, `<article>`,
or the largest child by text length) and process any `<pre><code class="language-map">` blocks.

```html
<div id="app">
  <main>
    <article class="dumby-block">
      <h2>Taipei</h2>
      <p>City centre: <a href="geo:25.0338,121.5645">25.0338, 121.5645</a></p>
      <pre><code class="language-map">
use: Maplibre
center: [121.5645, 25.0338]
zoom: 12
      </code></pre>
    </article>
  </main>
</div>
<script type="module">
  import { generateMaps } from 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/outdoorsafetylab/dumbymap@master/dist/dumbymap.mjs'
  generateMaps(document.getElementById('app'))
</script>
```


## Map blocks

Maps are declared as YAML inside a fenced code block tagged `map` (in Markdown)
or inside `<pre><code class="language-map">` (in HTML).

**Minimal example (Leaflet is the default renderer):**

```
```map
center: [121.5645, 25.0338]
zoom: 12
```
```

**Choosing a renderer:**

```yaml
use: Maplibre      # MapLibre GL JS
# use: Openlayers  # OpenLayers
# use: Leaflet     # Leaflet (default when `use` is omitted)
center: [121.5645, 25.0338]
zoom: 12
```

**Common YAML keys:**

| Key      | Type            | Description                                      |
|----------|-----------------|--------------------------------------------------|
| `use`    | string          | Renderer: `Maplibre`, `Openlayers`, `Leaflet`    |
| `id`     | string          | Explicit map ID (spaces become underscores)      |
| `center` | [lon, lat]      | Initial camera centre (WGS 84 by default)        |
| `zoom`   | number          | Initial zoom level                               |
| `bounds` | [[minLon,minLat],[maxLon,maxLat]] | Fit view to bounding box        |

**Multiple maps in one fenced block** — separate configs with `---`:

```
```map
id: north
use: Maplibre
center: [121.5645, 25.0338]
zoom: 7
---
id: south
use: Maplibre
center: [121.5645, 23.0338]
zoom: 7
```
```


## Content blocks

When the `markdown` option is used, Markdown is split into `.dumby-block` (`<article>`)
elements at every sequence of three or more consecutive newlines (`\n\n\n`).
Fenced code blocks are treated as atomic — blank lines inside a fence do not split.

When using plain HTML, DumbyMap auto-wraps content into blocks:
- `h1`/`h2`/`h3` headings start a new block.
- If no headings, `<section>`/`<article>` children are promoted.
- Otherwise everything is wrapped in one block.

Pre-existing elements with class `dumby-block` are used as-is.


## GeoLinks

GeoLinks are `<a href="geo:lat,lon">` anchors that move the map camera and place markers.

### Writing GeoLinks in Markdown/HTML

```html
<a href="geo:25.0338,121.5645">Taipei city centre</a>
```

Note: the `geo:` URI uses **lat,lon** order (standard RFC 5870).

### URL parameters on geo: links

| Parameter | Example              | Description                                 |
|-----------|----------------------|---------------------------------------------|
| `id`      | `?id=north,south`    | Target only the named map(s)                |
| `type`    | `?type=star`         | Marker icon type (default: `pin`)           |
| `text`    | `?text=My label`     | Custom leader-line midpoint label           |
| `crs`     | `?crs=EPSG:3826`     | Source CRS (projected to WGS 84 via proj4)  |

Example: `<a href="geo:25.0338,121.5645?id=north&type=campsite">Camp</a>`

### Auto-detected coordinate text

DumbyMap automatically converts plain text matching coordinate patterns into GeoLinks.
Supported formats:

- Decimal `lon,lat`: `121.5645,25.0338`
- Decimal with slash: `121.5645/25.0338`
- DMS: `E121d 30m 58s N25d 2m 50s`
- Unicode DMS: `E121度 30分 58秒 N25度 2分 50秒`
- Full-width Unicode digits: `１２１．５６４５，２５．０３３８`

Strings that look like dates are skipped to avoid false positives.

### Interaction

- **Click** a GeoLink: pan map to coordinate, place a marker.
- **Hover** a GeoLink: draw a leader line from link to marker.
- **Middle-click** a GeoLink: remove all its markers.
- **Right-click** a GeoLink: change marker type or delete the link.
- **Hold & drag** selected text onto a map: interactively create a GeoLink with a placed marker.


## DocLinks

DocLinks draw leader lines from an anchor to any CSS-selector-targeted element.

```markdown
[link text](#fragment "=> .css-selector")
```

The `href` is a normal fragment for page navigation.
The `title` attribute starting with `=>` provides the CSS selector for the leader line target.

Example: `[See the map](#map-section "=> .map-section")`

**Leader line path styles** (right-click to change):
`magnet` (default) | `straight` | `grid` | `fluid`


## Markers

Predefined icon types for GeoLinks:

| Category      | Icons                                                        |
|---------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| Basic         | `pin`, `circle`                                              |
| Recreation    | `campsite`, `mountain`, `waterfall`, `hotSpring`, `animal`   |
| Infrastructure| `parking`, `rail`, `heliport`, `racetrack`                   |
| Warning       | `caution`, `danger`, `cross`, `construction`, `fence`, `communication` |
| Shapes        | `star`, `triangle`, `triangleWhite`                          |
| Other         | `water`, `roadblock`                                         |

Set via `?type=<icon>` in the `geo:` URL, or at runtime via the context menu.
Right-click on any rendered map and choose **Add Marker** to drop a named pin manually.


## Layouts

Four built-in layouts control how the content column and the map showcase are arranged.
Switch with the **X** key (Shift+X to go back), or via the right-click context menu.

| Name          | Description                                                         |
|---------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `normal`      | Default; content and showcase arranged by CSS only                  |
| `side-by-side`| Draggable vertical splitter between content and maps (25%–75%)      |
| `overlay`     | Content blocks float as draggable, resizable panels over the maps   |
| `sticky`      | Maps float as a draggable panel fixed to the viewport               |

Set a layout programmatically:

```js
const dumbymap = generateMaps(container, { initialLayout: 'side-by-side' })
```

Sync with URL query parameter (reads `?layout=` on load, writes it on change):

```js
generateMaps(container, { markdown, urlParams: true })
```


## Coordinate Reference Systems

Default CRS is **WGS 84 (EPSG:4326)**. To accept coordinates in another projection,
set `data-crs` on the container element or pass `crs` in options:

```html
<div id="app" data-crs="EPSG:3826"></div>
```

```js
generateMaps(container, { crs: 'EPSG:3826' })
```

DumbyMap reprojects coordinates to WGS 84 via proj4 before panning the map.
Right-click any map → **Get Coordinates** to copy the projected coordinates at the click point.


## `generateMaps(container, options)` API

**Parameters:**

| Option           | Type             | Default      | Description                                              |
|------------------|------------------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
| `markdown`       | string           | —            | Markdown source; if omitted, reads from existing DOM     |
| `crs`            | string           | `EPSG:4326`  | Coordinate reference system for the container           |
| `initialLayout`  | string           | `normal`     | Starting layout name                                     |
| `layouts`        | array            | —            | Additional custom layout objects or name strings         |
| `urlParams`      | boolean          | `false`      | Read/write `?layout=` from/to URL query string           |
| `contentSelector`| string           | —            | CSS selector to resolve the content holder               |
| `renderCallback` | function         | —            | Called after each individual map finishes rendering      |
| `defaultApply`   | string (URL)     | remote YAML  | URL of YAML file providing default map option aliases    |

**Returns** a sealed `dumbymap` object:

```js
const dumbymap = generateMaps(container, options)

dumbymap.container       // root HTMLElement
dumbymap.htmlHolder      // live getter: .SemanticHtml element
dumbymap.showcase        // live getter: .Showcase element
dumbymap.blocks          // live getter: all .dumby-block elements
dumbymap.layouts         // array of Layout instances
dumbymap.aliases         // map option aliases loaded from defaultApply URL
dumbymap.modal           // PlainModal instance (used by block editor)
dumbymap.utils           // bound utility functions (see below)
```

**`dumbymap.utils` methods:**

```js
dumbymap.utils.renderedMaps()              // fulfilled .mapclay elements sorted by order
dumbymap.utils.focusNextMap(reverse?)      // cycle map focus (throttled)
dumbymap.utils.focusNextBlock(reverse?)    // cycle block focus
dumbymap.utils.removeBlockFocus()          // clear all block focus
dumbymap.utils.switchToNextLayout(reverse?)// cycle layout (throttled)
dumbymap.utils.scrollToBlock(block)        // smart-scroll a block into view
dumbymap.utils.addMarkerByPoint({point, map, title})  // place marker at screen pixel
dumbymap.utils.addGeoSchemeByText(node)    // scan text for coordinates, wrap as geo: links
dumbymap.utils.setContextMenu(callback)    // append custom items to the context menu
```


## Keyboard shortcuts

Shortcuts are disabled when a `<textarea>` or `<input>` has focus.

| Key         | Action                          |
|-------------|---------------------------------|
| `Tab`       | Focus next map                  |
| `Shift+Tab` | Focus previous map              |
| `x`         | Switch to next layout           |
| `Shift+X`   | Switch to previous layout       |
| `n`         | Focus next block                |
| `p`         | Focus previous block            |
| `Escape`    | Remove focus from all blocks    |


## DOM structure produced by generateMaps

```
<div id="app" class="Dumby" data-layout="normal" data-crs="EPSG:4326">
  <div class="SemanticHtml">
    <article class="dumby-block">
      <!-- content with <pre><code class="language-map"> map blocks -->
    </article>
    <!-- more .dumby-block elements -->
  </div>
  <div class="Showcase">
    <!-- focused .mapclay map elements are moved here -->
  </div>
</div>
```

Each `.mapclay` element gets:
- `id` — derived from `use:` renderer name (e.g. `Maplibre`, `Leaflet-2`)
- `data-render` — `"fulfilled"` when ready, `"pass/total"` steps during rendering
- `style.order` — CSS order reflecting DOM position


## Context menu

Right-click anywhere inside the container to open the context menu.
Disable it for a specific element with `data-menu="disabled"`.

Key actions available:
- **Edit Block** — opens a modal textarea pre-filled with the block's Markdown source; save with Ctrl+Enter.
- **Edit Map** — replace map with a YAML textarea; blur to re-render immediately.
- **Get Coordinates** — unprojects click position, copies `[lon, lat]` to clipboard.
- **Add Marker** — prompts for a name, places a pin at the click position.
- **Add Link by Geocoding** — select text, right-click, search Nominatim, insert a GeoLink.
- **Restore Camera** — returns map to its original position.
- **Marker Type** — change icon for a GeoLink.
- **Line Type** — change leader-line path style.
- **Layouts / Maps / Blocks** — navigation pickers (also reachable via keyboard).


## Markdown features

The Markdown pipeline (`md2dumbyBlocks`) uses markdown-it with these plugins active:

- `markdown-it-anchor` — heading IDs and `.header-anchor` links
- `markdown-it-footnote` — `[^1]` footnote syntax
- `markdown-it-front-matter` — YAML front matter (stripped from output)
- `markdown-it-inject-linenumbers` — `data-source-line` attributes on block elements
- `markdown-it-attrs` — `{.class #id attr=val}` attribute shorthand
- Raw HTML passthrough (`html: true`)

Block splitting: three or more consecutive blank lines (`\n\n\n`) create a new `.dumby-block`.
Fenced code blocks are atomic — blank lines inside a fence never trigger a split.
